audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:subject + gaat/gaan + infinitive]] and [[grammar:over + time span]]. Use [[grammar:gaan]] plus an infinitive for a plan that will happen later. Use [[grammar:over]] before a time span to say how long from now.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:subject + gaat/gaan + infinitive]]: The finite form of [[grammar:gaan]] carries person and number; the lexical infinitive closes the verbal bracket.; [[grammar:over + time span]]: The preposition [[grammar:over]] anchors the event prospectively relative to speech time.

audienceSections.normal:
#### subject + gaat/gaan + infinitive

Use [[grammar:gaan]] plus an infinitive for a plan that will happen later. Example: [[grammar:Ik ga vandaag de bestemming bevestigen.]]

#### over + time span

Use [[grammar:over]] before a time span to say how long from now. Example: [[grammar:Over twee weken vertrekt onze excursie.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### subject + gaat/gaan + infinitive

The finite form of [[grammar:gaan]] carries person and number; the lexical infinitive closes the verbal bracket. Literal example: [[grammar:Ik ga vandaag de bestemming bevestigen.]]

#### over + time span

The preposition [[grammar:over]] anchors the event prospectively relative to speech time. Literal example: [[grammar:Over twee weken vertrekt onze excursie.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Gaan]] plus an infinitive commonly expresses a plan. [[grammar:Over]] plus a duration looks forward from the present moment.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Gaan]] plus an infinitive commonly expresses a plan. [[grammar:Over]] plus a duration looks forward from the present moment. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Over twee weken vertrekt onze excursie.]] — Our excursion leaves in two weeks.
- [[grammar:Waar gaan we precies naartoe?]] — Where exactly are we going?
- [[grammar:We gaan het museum en de oude haven bezoeken.]] — We are going to visit the museum and the old harbor.
- [[grammar:Ik ga vandaag de bestemming bevestigen.]] — I am going to confirm the destination today.
- [[grammar:Hoeveel leerlingen staan op de deelnemerslijst?]] — How many pupils are on the participant list?
- [[grammar:Er staan honderdvijfentwintig namen op.]] — There are one hundred twenty-five names on it.
- [[grammar:Iedereen moet toestemming van thuis meenemen.]] — Everyone has to bring permission from home.
- [[grammar:Ik meld me vanmiddag aan.]] — I will register this afternoon.
- [[grammar:Dan ga ik jouw naam op de deelnemerslijst zetten.]] — Then I am going to put your name on the participant list.
- [[grammar:Over drie dagen sluiten we de inschrijving.]] — In three days we will close registration.
- [[grammar:Krijgt iedereen een vaste zitplaats?]] — Does everyone get an assigned seat?
- [[grammar:Ja, we gaan de zitplaatsen morgen verdelen.]] — Yes, we are going to allocate the seats tomorrow.
- [[grammar:Het vertrek is om half acht bij de school.]] — Departure is at half past seven by the school.
- [[grammar:Ik ga vroeg naar de halte fietsen.]] — I am going to cycle to the stop early.
- [[grammar:Neem een lunchdoos en genoeg water mee.]] — Bring a lunch box and enough water.
- [[grammar:Over twee weken is alles klaar.]] — In two weeks everything will be ready.
- [[grammar:Mooi, ik ga het thuis meteen vertellen.]] — Great, I am going to tell them at home right away.
- [[grammar:En morgen bevestigen we het hele plan.]] — And tomorrow we will confirm the whole plan.
